exam_format: Computer-based (written); In-person practical at authorized site

testing_centers.notes: Exams are administered at authorized NIC testing sites, often coordinated through state boards of cosmetology. Registration and scheduling handled at nictest.com or through the candidate's state board. Practical exams require a mannequin and kit supplied by the candidate per state board specifications.

id_requirements: Government-issued photo ID required. Name must match registration. Some states require proof of school enrollment or graduation hours.
exam_rules: No personal items, notes, or electronic devices permitted during written exam. Practical exams require candidate-supplied kit; allowed tools and mannequin specifications vary by state. Written exam is computer-based at authorized test site.
renewal_options: Cosmetology licenses are issued by state boards after passing NIC (or equivalent) exams. License renewal periods and continuing education requirements vary by state; typically 2-year renewal cycles with CEU requirements. Candidates must contact their state board for renewal details.

retake_policy.notes: Retake policies are set by individual state boards. Most states impose a waiting period between failed attempts (commonly 30–90 days). Full exam fee typically required for each retake. Check your state board for specifics.

official_study_resources.title: NIC Candidate Information Bulletin
official_study_resources.notes: Candidate information bulletins and content outlines available at nictest.com
